persons.arguments_against: The strongest counter-hypothesis is a same-name relative or successor: another A. G. Lascelles serving in Ceylon's Judicial Establishment, with the corpus holding 4 officials and 3 biographies under this surname. This fails because the stint is not a generic junior post but the unique offices of Attorney-General and Chief Justice of Ceylon, held in exactly the years the biography gives (atty.-gen. 1902/1906, chief just. 1911); only one person can hold the Chief Justiceship at a time. The stint's final year (1914) matches the biography's 'retired, 1914.' exactly, and the staff list adds 'Sir' in 1914, consistent with the 1913 knighthood (Kt., 1913). A second hypothesis — that the 'place_inherited' FAIL means the Ceylon location is unreliable — fails because the verbatim entry explicitly says 'atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902', so the colony is directly attested, not merely inherited.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second Alfred G. Lascelles in Ceylon's judicial service in 1905-1914 (e.g. a distinct staff-list line or biography showing different birth year or career), or a record showing the Chief Justice of Ceylon 1911-1914 was a different person.

notes: Multiple independent agreeing dimensions: both initials, unique senior offices (Attorney-General then Chief Justice of Ceylon) with matching years, knighthood timing (Kt 1913 -> 'Sir' in 1914 staff list), and stint terminating exactly at the biography's retirement year 1914.
